pattern: A counting argument settles feasibility: equal-sized distinct groups exist exactly when the count divides evenly and no value appears more often than the number of groups.
hints: If the total count is not a multiple of the group size, no valid split exists at all.
Two copies of the same value can never share a group, so each duplicate must go to a different group.
Let g be the number of groups (total over group size). The answer is true precisely when the most frequent value occurs at most g times.

edge_inputs.description: a duplicate spread across two groups
k equals length, one group of all distinct
k of 1 always works since singletons are distinct
divisible length but a value appears too many times
exactly g copies of one value is allowed

stress.description: random multiset, k a random divisor candidate of length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, max(2, n // 3)) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: small value range forces frequent duplicates so both branches trigger
